paper_title: GPNAS: A Neural Network Architecture Search Framework Based on Graphical Predictor
problem_statement: Existing Neural Architecture Search (NAS) methods have various defects, including high computational cost (evolutionary algorithms), failure to solve credit allocation (RL and gradient-based methods), reliance on manual priors (BOHB), search-space transplantation leading to overfitting (differentiable NAS), large noise in search analysis (alternate RL search), and limited versatility of predictors (LSTM-based predictor). These issues force compromises in practical applications.
proposed_solution: The authors propose GPNAS, a framework that decouples the search space for skip-connections and operators. It replaces the RL searcher with BOHB for improved efficiency and utilizes a GCN-based (Graph Convolutional Network) predictor.
key_contributions: An alternate neural network search framework that decouples the search space of skip-connection and operator, using BOHB instead of RL to improve efficiency.

related_work.critique: Require a large amount of computation.
Reduced calculation cost but failed to solve the problem of credit allocation.
Reduced calculation cost but failed to solve the problem of credit allocation.
Relies on manual priors and requires strong model design ability from the engineer, which defeats the original purpose of NAS.
The searched cell structure is not directly applied to the target network, leading to a mismatch between training and evaluation goals and potential overfitting.

related_work.method: Alternate search model based on RL for decoupled search spaces (Jiang [20])
related_work.critique: Analysis of the skip-connection search path has large noise, and searching for skip-connection using RL is considered inefficient.

related_work.method: A predictor using LSTM for progressive growth (Liu [25])
related_work.critique: Limited to predicting the performance of child models without skip-connections, which greatly limits its versatility.

title: GPNAS: A Neural Network Architecture Search Framework Based on Graphical Predictor
